derived_from: FIPS 180-4 — Secure Hash Standard (SHS), NIST, August 2015
RFC 1321 — The MD5 Message-Digest Algorithm, April 1992
Test vectors verified by running OpenSSL 3.6.1 on macOS/Darwin 25.3.0, 2026-03-31
Cross-checked against Python 3.14.3 hashlib module (see zspecs/hashlib.zspec.json)
notes: This spec exercises the openssl CLI tool via subprocess. It is a CLI-backend spec — the 'library' is the installed openssl binary, not a shared library or Python module.

Output format of 'openssl dgst': the algorithm prefix varies by OpenSSL version and fork. OpenSSL 3.x uses 'SHA2-256', older versions use 'SHA256', LibreSSL uses 'SHA256'. The hex digest value is always present in the output regardless of prefix. All hash invariants use cli_stdout_contains on the hex digest only.

Cross-spec interoperability: the hash values in this spec are identical to those in zspecs/hashlib.zspec.json. This is a fundamental invariant: openssl and Python hashlib must produce the same output for the same input on the same algorithm. They do.
The 'openssl dgst -r' (raw) format outputs '<hexdigest> *stdin' — useful for piping but less human-readable.
SHA-256('abc') = ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad — same value verified in hashlib.zspec.json provenance notes.

library.notes: openssl must be in PATH. The spec targets 'openssl dgst' subcommand. Compatible with OpenSSL >= 1.1.0 and LibreSSL >= 2.5.

exit_codes.name: EXIT_FAILURE
exit_codes.description: openssl exits 1 on failure (bad subcommand, invalid options)

hex_digest.description: A lowercase hexadecimal string produced by openssl dgst. Length depends on algorithm: SHA-256=64, SHA-1=40, MD5=32, SHA-512=128.

openssl_version.description: Print the OpenSSL version string to stdout and exit 0.

openssl_dgst.description: Compute a message digest of stdin (or a file). The algorithm is selected by a flag: -sha256, -sha1, -md5, -sha512, etc.

parameters.name: -r
parameters.description: If present, output raw: '<hexdigest> *stdin' instead of the labelled format.

parameters.name: file...
parameters.description: Optional input files. If absent, reads from stdin.

postconditions: stdout contains the hex digest of the input

invariants.description: openssl version stdout contains 'SSL' — works for both 'OpenSSL' and 'LibreSSL'
SHA-256 of empty input == e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855 — cross-checks hashlib.sha256.empty
SHA-256 of b'abc' == ba7816bf... — cross-checks hashlib.sha256.abc
SHA-256 of 56-byte FIPS 180-4 Appendix B message — cross-checks hashlib.sha256.fips_message2
SHA-1 of empty input == da39a3ee5e6b4b0d3255bfef95601890afd80709 — cross-checks hashlib.sha1.empty
SHA-1 of b'abc' == a9993e364706816aba3e25717850c26c9cd0d89d — cross-checks hashlib.sha1.abc
MD5 of empty input == d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e — cross-checks hashlib.md5.empty
MD5 of the quick-brown-fox string == 9e107d9d372bb6826bd81d3542a419d6 — cross-checks hashlib.md5.quick_brown_fox
SHA-512 of empty input — cross-checks hashlib.sha512.empty
openssl dgst -sha256 -r outputs '<hexdigest> *stdin' on a single line (raw format, no algorithm prefix)
openssl dgst -sha256 exits 0 on valid input
openssl with an invalid subcommand exits 1
openssl with an invalid subcommand writes an error message to stderr

dgst_labelled_output.description: Default output format of openssl dgst: '<ALGORITHM>(<source>)= <hexdigest>\n'. The ALGORITHM prefix varies: 'SHA256', 'SHA2-256', or 'MD5' depending on OpenSSL version.

fields.name: algorithm_label
fields.description: Algorithm name followed by source in parentheses, e.g. 'SHA2-256(stdin)'

fields.name: hex_digest
fields.description: Lowercase hex representation of the digest

dgst_raw_output.description: Raw output format (openssl dgst -r): '<hexdigest> *<source>\n'. The '*' indicates binary mode.

error_model.return_code_semantics: openssl exits 0 on success, 1 on failure. Error messages go to stderr.
error_model.error_stickiness: N/A — CLI tool, each invocation is independent.

error_codes.name: EXIT_FAILURE
error_codes.meaning: Command failed: invalid subcommand, bad options, or input error. Error message written to stderr.
